name: French Polynesia (Polynésie française / Pōrīnetia Farāni)
summary: French Polynesia is a Pacific Island civil-law overseas collectivity (Collectivité d'outre-mer / COM) of the French Republic with substantial autonomy under the Organic Law 2004-192 of 27 February 2004 (Statute of Autonomy). Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code (Code Civil) with local-law adaptations and customary-land jurisdiction (statut de droit civil de droit commun + statut de droit local). Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387. The Court of Appeal of Papeete is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Tribunal of First Instance of Papeete (Tribunal de Première Instance) and on customary-land matters via the Tribunal de Terre. Psychology profession is regulated through the French Republic professional framework as applicable in French Polynesia. French Polynesia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. French Polynesia is a Hague Convention 1980 party via French Republic territorial extension effective 1 December 1983.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in French Polynesia)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applicable in French Polynesia. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Organic Law 2004-192 of 27 February 2004 (Statute of Autonomy)
statutory_framework.title: Statute of Autonomy
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Organic Law establishing French Polynesia's substantial autonomy with local-law adaptation competence.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Papeete (Cour d'appel de Papeete)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from French Polynesia.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in French Polynesia)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in French Polynesia.

anonymisation_convention: French Polynesian family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: French protectorate establishment + Tahiti annexation framework
key_developments.description: French protectorate over Tahiti established 9 September 1842 — substantively significant Pacific colonial-protectorate framework. Subsequent annexation 1880 establishing French-colony status. Foundational colonial-framework for contemporary French-Republic-territorial-administration.

key_developments.title: Overseas territory status 1946 + post-WWII framework
key_developments.description: French Polynesia (then 'French Establishments in Oceania') became Overseas Territory (Territoire d'outre-mer) of French Republic 1946. Substantive constitutional-restructuring affecting post-WWII Pacific French territorial-administration framework.

key_developments.title: Centre d'Expérimentation du Pacifique + nuclear-testing era
key_developments.description: Centre d'Expérimentation du Pacifique (CEP) established 1962 — nuclear testing at Moruroa and Fangataufa atolls 1966-1996 (193 tests total). Substantively distinctive Pacific nuclear-testing pattern affecting demographic, environmental and family-law-implementation framework. Subsequent compensation framework Loi Morin 2010.

key_developments.title: Statute of Autonomy 1977 + Pouvanaa a Oopa-political-legacy framework
key_developments.description: Statute of Autonomy 1977 (Law 77-772) substantively reforming territorial-political framework — first-stage-autonomy framework. Subsequent enhanced autonomy 1984. Foundational pre-2004-Statute-of-Autonomy framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by France to French Polynesia effective 1 December 1983 — establishing Pacific Hague Convention jurisdiction framework within French Republic territorial framework.

key_developments.title: France ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Polynesia extension
key_developments.description: France ratified the UNCRC on 7 August 1990 with extension to French Polynesia — establishing best-interests-of-the-child substantive doctrine within French-civil-law overseas-collectivity framework.

key_developments.title: End of nuclear testing + Loi Morin 2010 compensation framework
key_developments.description: Nuclear testing ended January 1996 — substantive trajectory toward compensation framework. Loi Morin 5 January 2010 (Law 2010-2) establishing compensation framework for nuclear-test victims. Subsequent reforms 2013, 2017 substantively expanding compensation framework affecting family-law-implementation (intergenerational health impacts).

key_developments.title: Statute of Autonomy (Organic Law 2004-192)
key_developments.description: Federal Organic Law of 27 February 2004 establishing French Polynesia's substantial autonomy with local-law adaptation competence — substantively distinctive within French-overseas-collectivity framework. Substantive devolution of competences affecting family-law-cross-cutting-areas.

key_developments.title: Re-listing on UN Decolonization List 2013 + decolonisation-discourse framework
key_developments.description: UN General Assembly Resolution 67/265 (17 May 2013) re-listed French Polynesia on UN Decolonization List — substantively distinctive international-decolonisation-discourse framework affecting French-Republic-territorial-administration. Substantively contested status-framework persisting through 2020s.

key_developments.title: Brotherson presidency + Tāvini Huira'atira independence-trajectory + DSFE framework
key_developments.description: Moetai Brotherson (Tāvini Huira'atira) presidency 12 May 2023-onwards framework — substantively distinctive Pacific French-COM independence-trajectory framework. Direction des Solidarités, de la Famille et de l'Égalité (DSFE) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Civil Code + Statute of Autonomy 2004 framework. Substantively significant French Pacific COM institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour d'appel de Papeete + Cour de Cassation — interest-of-the-child substantive register
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Papeete and French Court of Cassation continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Statute of Autonomy 2004 framework in custody disputes without adopting the 'parental alienation' label as a doctrinal term. Substantive analysis within French-civil-law-overseas-collectivity + customary-land-Tribunal-de-Terre framework.

structural_findings: French Polynesia operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with substantial autonomy and customary-land (Tribunal de Terre) parallel jurisdiction — places French Polynesia in the French overseas collectivity cluster within the Pacific.
Statute of Autonomy (2004) is structurally distinctive within French overseas territories — substantial local-law adaptation competence.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via French Republic territorial extension reflects overseas-collectivity Hague jurisdiction status.
Bilingual official-language framework (French + Tahitian) reflects Pacific Polynesian linguistic heritage.
editorial_notes: French Polynesia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 2 to 10 key_developments with full protectorate-to-contemporary trajectory: 1842-French-protectorate-+-Tahiti-annexation + 1946-Overseas-territory-status-+-post-WWII + 1966-Centre-d'Expérimentation-du-Pacifique-+-nuclear-testing-era + 1977-Statute-of-Autonomy-1977 + 1983-Hague-Convention-1980-territorial-extension + 1990-UNCRC-+-Polynesia-extension + 1996-end-of-nuclear-testing-+-Loi-Morin-2010-compensation + 2004-Statute-of-Autonomy-Organic-Law-2004-192 + 2013-Re-listing-on-UN-Decolonization-List + 2024-Cour-d'appel-de-Papeete-+-Cour-de-Cassation-interest-of-the-child.
Civil-law Pacific French overseas collectivity (French Civil Code + Statute of Autonomy 2004 + Tribunal de Terre customary-land + bilingual + Hague via French Republic territorial extension 1983).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + customary-land-Tribunal-de-Terre framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Pacific-Island + French-civil-law + French-overseas-collectivity-cluster + Centre-d'Expérimentation-du-Pacifique-nuclear-testing-193-tests-Moruroa-Fangataufa-1966-1996-distinctive-Pacific-pattern + Loi-Morin-2010-compensation-framework + Statute-of-Autonomy-2004-substantial-autonomy-distinctive + UN-Decolonization-List-2013-re-listing-contested-status + customary-land-Tribunal-de-Terre + Hague-via-French-Republic-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
